message:
fix(exec): replace blocking thread joins with deadline-based drain loops for process I/O

The previous implementation of exec/exec_ref host calls joined the
stdout and stderr reader threads after the child process exited. When
a reader thread blocked indefinitely (e.g. inherited file descriptors
kept a pipe open, or the reader hit a pathological UTF-8 boundary),
the join would hang forever and the extension dispatch would never
return.

This change replaces the blocking join pattern with a streaming
channel-based approach across all three exec call-sites:

extension_dispatcher.rs (exec_ref):
  - Replace join() with a 5-second deadline drain loop that polls
    is_finished() on both reader thread handles and breaks on timeout

extensions.rs (exec_ref with limit):
  - Migrate from read_to_end_capped_and_drain to a pump_stream model
    using mpsc::sync_channel<ExecStreamFrame>
  - Accumulate stdout/stderr incrementally via try_recv between child
    process polls, with recv_timeout during wait loops
  - Add 2-second post-exit drain deadline for channel flush
  - Explicitly drop rx_stream to unblock pump threads, then reap child

extensions_js.rs (QuickJS exec bridge):
  - Same pump_stream + sync_channel migration as extensions.rs but
    using a local StreamChunk struct and ingest_chunk macro
  - Remove unused __pi_crypto_sha256_hex_native binding (dead code
    after the crypto module was consolidated)
  - Remove unused AtomicBool import

The net effect is that process exec calls now have bounded completion
time even when reader threads misbehave, preventing agent hangs during
tool execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
